mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easier discounting and less policy drag on risk assets, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 20 trading days when the policy rate is in decline.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in more than 55% of months with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating while FEDFUNDS remains elevated, real policy tightness rises and equity valuation multiples compress, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change rises above 4% while FEDFUNDS is above 4%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the signal loses sign consistency for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE indicates labor-market deterioration and slower consumer income growth, so equity returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days after the unemployment rate crosses higher by a meaningful margin.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not below the unconditional median after UNRATE rises 0.3pp in 3 months, or if the hit rate is not at least 55% negative across triggered events.

mechanism_narrative: A broad pickup in PAYEMS and RSAFS implies stronger payroll income and retail spending, which supports earnings expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous monthly gains in PAYEMS and RSAFS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the spread versus baseline returns is not statistically positive.

mechanism_narrative: An improvement in INDPRO after a prior soft patch signals better industrial supply conditions and firmer cyclical activity, so S&P 500 returns should be positive over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month change turns positive after a prior 2-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO turnarounds do not lead to positive 5-day SPX returns at least 55% of the time, or if the effect disappears in two separate expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a forward demand barometer; a sustained rise in HOUST lifts confidence in construction and household spending, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward SPX returns are not above baseline following three-month HOUST uptrends, or if the sign is negative in more than half of such episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A joint deterioration in PAYEMS and rising UNRATE captures simultaneous payroll loss and slack creation, which signals recession risk and should depress S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up condition is followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the interaction term is not negative in rolling validation.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle inflation regime, a simultaneous rise in CPIAUCSL and elevated FEDFUNDS means real rates are restrictive while inflation remains sticky, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month inflation above 3% and FEDFUNDS above 4% while UNRATE is at or above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes deliver positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the model coefficient on the CPIAUCSL-FEDFUNDS interaction is not stably negative across rolling windows.
